# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create Docker Compose configuration for ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana). Define services for log collection, storage, and visualization. Configure Logstash pipelines for log parsing. Expose Kibana UI and Elasticsearch ports for access. This is for our centralized logging platform.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for elasticsearch logs exposed and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: elasticsearch_logs_exposed

version: '3.8'

services:
  elasticsearch:
    image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.0
    container_name: elasticsearch
    environment:
      - node.name=es-node-01
      - cluster.name=elk-cluster
      - discovery.type=single-node
      - bootstrap.memory_lock=true
      - xpack.security.enabled=true
      - xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled=false
      - xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled=false
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeStrongPass123!}
      - "ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms1g -Xmx1g"
      - xpack.security.audit.enabled=true
      - logger.level=WARN
      - logger.org.elasticsearch.deprecation=ERROR
    ulimits:
      memlock:
        soft: -1
        hard: -1
      nofile:
        soft: 65536
        hard: 65536
    volumes:
      - elasticsearch-data:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data
    networks:
      - elk-internal
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:9200:9200"
    healthcheck:
      test: >
        curl -s -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeStrongPass123!}
        http://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | grep -q '"status":"green"\|"status":"yellow"'
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 60s
    restart: unless-stopped
    logging:
      driver: "json-file"
      options:
        max-size: "10m"
        max-file: "3"
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 2g
        reservations:
          memory: 1g

  logstash:
    image: docker.elastic.co/logstash/logstash:8.11.0
    container_name: logstash
    environment:
      - "LS_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
      - xpack.monitoring.enabled=true
      - xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.hosts=http://elasticsearch:9200
      - xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.username=elastic
      - xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.password=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeStrongPass123!}
      - LOG_LEVEL=warn
    volumes:
      - ./logstash/pipeline:/usr/share/logstash/pipeline:ro
      - ./logstash/config/logstash.yml:/usr/share/logstash/config/logstash.yml:ro
      - logstash-data:/usr/share/logstash/data
    networks:
      - elk-internal
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:5044:5044"
      - "127.0.0.1:5000:5000/tcp"
      - "127.0.0.1:5000:5000/udp"
      - "127.0.0.1:9600:9600"
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: >
        curl -s http://localhost:9600/_node/stats | grep -q '"status":"green"\|"status":"yellow"'
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 60s
    restart: unless-stopped
    logging:
      driver: "json-file"
      options:
        max-size: "10m"
        max-file: "3"
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 1g
        reservations:
          memory: 512m

  kibana:
    image: docker.elastic.co/kibana/kibana:8.11.0
    container_name: kibana
    environment:
      - ELASTICSEARCH_HOSTS=http://elasticsearch:9200
      - ELASTICSEARCH_USERNAME=kibana_system
      - ELASTICSEARCH_PASSWORD=${KIBANA_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeKibanaPass123!}
      - xpack.security.enabled=true
      - xpack.encryptedSavedObjects.encryptionKey=${KIBANA_ENCRYPTION_KEY:-a]3@mN!9pQ2rS$tU5vW^xY7zA1bC3dE4f}
      - server.host=0.0.0.0
      - server.name=kibana
      - logging.root.level=warn
      - csp.strict=true
      - csp.warnLegacyBrowsers=true
    networks:
      - elk-internal
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:5601:5601"
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: >
        curl -s http://localhost:5601/api/status | grep -q '"level":"available"'
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 90s
    restart: unless-stopped
    logging:
      driver: "json-file"
      options:
        max-size: "10m"
        max-file: "3"
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 1g
        reservations:
          memory: 512m

  setup:
    image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.0
    container_name: elk-setup
    networks:
      - elk-internal
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
    environment:
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeStrongPass123!}
      - KIBANA_PASSWORD=${KIBANA_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeKibanaPass123!}
    command: >
      bash -c '
        echo "Setting up kibana_system user password...";
        until curl -s -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeStrongPass123!}" \
          -X POST "http://elasticsearch:9200/_security/user/kibana_system/_password" \
          -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
          -d "{\"password\":\"${KIBANA_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeKibanaPass123!}\"}" | grep -q "^{}"; do
          sleep 5;
        done;
        echo "Creating logstash_writer role...";
        curl -s -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeStrongPass123!}" \
          -X POST "http://elasticsearch:9200/_security/role/logstash_writer" \
          -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
          -d "{
            \"cluster\": [\"manage_index_templates\", \"monitor\", \"manage_ilm\"],
            \"indices\": [{
              \"names\": [\"logstash-*\", \"syslog-*\", \"nginx-*\", \"app-*\"],
              \"privileges\": [\"write\", \"create\", \"create_index\", \"manage\", \"manage_ilm\"]
            }]
          }";
        echo "";
        echo "Creating logstash_internal user...";
        curl -s -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeStrongPass123!}" \
          -X POST "http://elasticsearch:9200/_security/user/logstash_internal" \
          -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
          -d "{
            \"password\": \"${LOGSTASH_INTERNAL_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeLogstashPass123!}\",
            \"roles\": [\"logstash_writer\"],
            \"full_name\": \"Logstash Internal User\"
          }";
        echo "";
        echo "Setup complete!";
      '
    restart: "no"
    logging:
      driver: "json-file"
      options:
        max-size: "5m"
        max-file: "1"

volumes:
  elasticsearch-data:
    driver: local
  logstash-data:
    driver: local

networks:
  elk-internal:
    driver: bridge
    internal: false

# --- Logstash Configuration Files ---
# Create the following files in your project directory:

# File: logstash/config/logstash.yml
# ---
# http.host: "0.0.0.0"
# xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.hosts: ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
# xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.username: "elastic"
# xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.password: "${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}"
# log.level: warn
# pipeline.ordered: auto
# ---

# File: logstash/pipeline/logstash.conf
# ---
# input {
#   beats {
#     port => 5044
#     ssl => false
#   }
#
#   tcp {
#     port => 5000
#     codec => json_lines
#     tags => ["tcp_input"]
#   }
#
#   udp {
#     port => 5000
#     codec => json
#     tags => ["udp_input"]
#   }
# }
#
# filter {
#   # Parse syslog messages
#   if [type] == "syslog" or "syslog" in [tags] {
#     grok {
#       match => {
#         "message" => "%{SYSLOGTIMESTAMP:syslog_timestamp} %{SYSLOGHOST:syslog_hostname} %{DATA:syslog_program}(?:\[%{POSINT:syslog_pid}\])?: %{GREEDYDATA:syslog_message}"
#       }
#       add_field => [ "received_at", "%{@timestamp}" ]
#       add_field => [ "received_from", "%{host}" ]
#       tag_on_failure => ["_syslog_parse_failure"]
#     }
#     date {
#       match => [ "syslog_timestamp", "MMM  d HH:mm:ss", "MMM dd HH:mm:ss" ]
#       target => "@timestamp"
#     }
#     # Remove sensitive fields that might leak into logs
#     mutate {
#       remove_field => ["password", "secret", "token", "api_key", "authorization"]
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Parse nginx access logs
#   if [type] == "nginx-access" or "nginx" in [tags] {
#     grok {
#       match => {
#         "message" => '%{IPORHOST:remote_addr} - %{DATA:remote_user} \[%{HTTPDATE:time_local}\] "%{WORD:request_method} %{DATA:request_uri} HTTP/%{NUMBER:http_version}" %{NUMBER:status} %{NUMBER:body_bytes_sent} "%{DATA:http_referer}" "%{DATA:http_user_agent}"'
#       }
#       tag_on_failure => ["_nginx_parse_failure"]
#     }
#     date {
#       match => [ "time_local", "dd/MMM/yyyy:HH:mm:ss Z" ]
#       target => "@timestamp"
#     }
#     # Anonymize IP addresses for privacy compliance
#     if [remote_addr] {
#       mutate {
#         copy => { "remote_addr" => "remote_addr_original" }
#       }
#     }
#     geoip {
#       source => "remote_addr"
#       target => "geoip"
#     }
#     useragent {
#       source => "http_user_agent"
#       target => "user_agent"
#     }
#     mutate {
#       convert => {
#         "status" => "integer"
#         "body_bytes_sent" => "integer"
#       }
#       # Strip query parameters that might contain sensitive data
#       gsub => [
#         "request_uri", "(?i)(password|token|secret|api_key|auth)=[^&]*", "\1=REDACTED"
#       ]
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Parse JSON application logs
#   if [type] == "app-json" or "app" in [tags] {
#     json {
#       source => "message"
#       target => "app"
#       tag_on_failure => ["_json_parse_failure"]
#     }
#     # Sanitize sensitive data from application logs
#     mutate {
#       remove_field => [
#         "[app][password]", "[app][secret]", "[app][token]",
#         "[app][api_key]", "[app][authorization]", "[app][credit_card]",
#         "[app][ssn]", "[app][private_key]"
#       ]
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Common enrichment for all logs
#   mutate {
#     add_field => {
#       "[@metadata][pipeline]" => "elk-centralized-logging"
#       "environment" => "${ENVIRONMENT:production}"
#     }
#     # Remove potentially sensitive metadata
#     remove_field => ["[beat][hostname]"]
#   }
#
#   # Drop debug-level logs in production to reduce noise
#   if [level] == "DEBUG" or [log_level] == "debug" {
#     drop { }
#   }
#
#   # Fingerprint for deduplication
#   fingerprint {
#     source => "message"
#     target => "[@metadata][fingerprint]"
#     method => "MURMUR3"
#   }
# }
#
# output {
#   if "_syslog_parse_failure" not in [tags] and "_nginx_parse_failure" not in [tags] and "_json_parse_failure" not in [tags] {
#     elasticsearch {
#       hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#       user => "logstash_internal"
#       password => "${LOGSTASH_INTERNAL_PASSWORD}"
#       index => "%{[@metadata][beat]:logstash}-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
#       document_id => "%{[@metadata][fingerprint]}"
#       ilm_enabled => true
#       ilm_rollover_alias => "logstash"
#       ilm_pattern => "000001"
#       ilm_policy => "logstash-policy"
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Route nginx logs to dedicated index
#   if "nginx" in [tags] and "_nginx_parse_failure" not in [tags] {
#     elasticsearch {
#       hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#       user => "logstash_internal"
#       password => "${LOGSTASH_INTERNAL_PASSWORD}"
#       index => "nginx-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Route failed parses to dead letter index for investigation
#   if "_syslog_parse_failure" in [tags] or "_nginx_parse_failure" in [tags] or "_json_parse_failure" in [tags] {
#     elasticsearch {
#       hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#       user => "logstash_internal"
#       password => "${LOGSTASH_INTERNAL_PASSWORD}"
#       index => "parse-failures-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
#     }
#   }
# }
# ---

# File: .env (create this file with your actual secrets - DO NOT commit to version control)
# ---
# ELASTIC_PASSWORD=YourStrongElasticPassword123!
# KIBANA_PASSWORD=YourStrongKibanaPassword456!
# LOGSTASH_INTERNAL_PASSWORD=YourStrongLogstashPassword789!
# KIBANA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=a-random-32-character-or-longer-string-here
# ENVIRONMENT=production
# ---
